Home / Alternatives to Monday.com / Monday.com vs Plane

Monday.com vs Plane

A side-by-side look at Monday.com (the paid SaaS) and Plane (the open source alternative). Use this page to decide if the switch fits your team and workflow.

Monday.com Plane
Tagline Work OS with flexible boards and automations. Open source JIRA alternative with cycles and modules.
License Proprietary SaaS AGPL-3.0
Pricing Free trial; Basic from $9/user/month (3-seat minimum). Free to self-host · optional paid hosted plan
Self-host option No Yes — difficulty 3/5
Hosted cloud available Yes (only option) Yes
Desktop apps Varies by product Web only
Mobile apps Official apps typically available None official
Ad slot — between tables

Best for

Teams who like Monday but want self-host.

Plane strengths

  • Modern UI inspired by Linear/Jira.
  • Cycles, modules and views.
  • Active community and rapid updates.

Plane weaknesses

  • Still adding features at pace — occasional rough edges.
  • Self-hosted setup involves several services.
  • Fewer integrations than mature incumbents.

What's the catch with Monday.com?

  • Minimum-seat pricing inflates costs for small teams.
  • Can feel bloated.
  • No self-hosted option.

Still unsure?

Check the full list of alternatives to Monday.com: see Monday.com alternatives, or learn more about Plane on its project page.

OpenInstead — open source alternatives to the SaaS you already use. Updated April 2026.
Disclaimer: licensing, features and pricing change. Verify each tool's current status on its official site before committing.
